description: Pre-stuffed assistant turn with unclosed <think> tag — can the model still respond correctly to the next user message?

messages.content: You are a coding assistant. Use tools to complete tasks.
Read the file config.py and then tell me the value of DEBUG.

<think>
The user wants me to read config.py. I should use the read tool.

function.name: read
function.description: Read a file; returns contents with line numbers.

function.name: edit
function.description: Replace an exact string in a file (first occurrence).

expect.notes: Target quirk (section 4.4 failure mode 1): when an assistant turn contains an unclosed <think> tag (no </think>) followed by a tool call, models that process thinking tokens may treat all subsequent history — including the tool result — as part of an ongoing reasoning block. Failure mode: model produces confused output, repeats the read call already done, ignores the tool result content, or generates a response that doesn't reference 'DEBUG = False' from the tool result. The pre-stuffed unclosed <think> in the assistant content is the trigger. Correct behavior: model ignores or handles the malformed tag and emits edit(path='config.py', old_str='DEBUG = False', new_str='DEBUG = True').
